t: Brain MRI — vascular, atrophy, other pathology.
Biomarkers (if clinical suspicion) — CSF amyloid-β/tau, PET imaging (Alzheimer's prodrome).
Affective symptoms are dominant; improves with antidepressants.
Modifiable factors (cardiovascular risk, B12, thyroid, depression, medication).
– physical activity (aerobic), Mediterranean diet, cognitive stimulation, social connections (FINGER trial Lancet 2015 – multimodal approach effective).
Pharmacotherapy — NO specific approval for MCI
. Donepezil and other AChEIs — limited evidence base; not routinely recommended.
— new anti-amyloid antibodies; FDA 2023 approval for Alzheimer prodrome and mild dementia; clinical effect modest, infusion reactions and ARIA (Amyloid-Related Imaging Abnormalities) risk.
Monitoring risk of transition to dementia
Lecanemab — CLARITY-AD (van Dyck NEJM 2023); donanemab — TRAILBLAZER-ALZ 2 (Sims JAMA 2023).
FINGER Multimodal Intervention (FINGER — Finnish Geriatric Intervention Study)
Diet + exercise + cognitive training + vascular risk management.
Anti-amyloid antibodies; FDA approval; ARIA monitoring (MRI).
Amnestic MCI as Alzheimer's prodrome — high conversion rate.
annual rate 10–15%; some remain stable or improve.
Myth 2: "Vitamin B and ginkgo cure MCI"
B vitamins only in deficiency; ginkgo Cochrane (Birks 2009) — weak evidence of effect.
Myth 3: "AChEI is the standard treatment for MCI"
AAN 2018 — evidence base insufficient; not routinely recommended.
Reduces clinical decline rate by ~25%; ARIA and infusion reactions; not "cure."
Myth 5: "Cognitive decline is inevitable with age"
Cardiovascular risk, physical activity, social connections are modifiable factors.
Petersen R.C. et al. Practice guideline update summary: Mild cognitive impairment. Neurology 2018;90(3):126–135.
Ngandu T. et al. A 2-year multidomain intervention of diet, exercise, cognitive training, and vascular risk monitoring (FINGER): a randomised controlled trial. Lancet 2015;385(9984):2255–2263.
van Dyck C.H. et al. Lecanemab in Early Alzheimer's Disease. NEJM 2023;388(1):9–21.
